feedback.issue: The test asserts `assert_not dark_mode?` immediately after sign-in, assuming the browser's default color-scheme preference is light, but never emulates/pins `prefers-color-scheme` for the Capybara/Selenium session. On a host (or CI runner) whose OS appearance is set to Dark, headless Chrome reports `prefers-color-scheme: dark`, the app correctly applies dark mode per its 'default to system settings' requirement, and this assertion fails (confirmed reproducing on this machine: `defaults read -g AppleInterfaceStyle` = Dark). Fix by explicitly forcing a known color scheme for the test session (e.g. a Chrome option/CDP call to set `prefers-color-scheme: light` in test/application_system_test_case.rb) so the test is deterministic regardless of the host's OS theme.
